behavior: Ground-forages for seeds, taking cover in shrubs; often forms loose flocks outside breeding season and gives a long, dry trill from elevated perches.
field_marks: Small, slender sparrow with a fairly long tail and a small conical bill.
Bright rufous to chestnut crown in breeding adults; browner and subdued outside breeding season.
Crisp dark line through the eye, set against a pale gray face.
Clean, unstreaked pale gray underparts in adults.
Brown-and-black streaked back and wings; gray rump is visible in flight.
Bill is dark in breeding plumage and often pinkish in nonbreeding plumage.
habitat: Open woodlands and forest edges with grassy clearings; also park-like tree-and-grass settings, shrubby borders, and gardens with trees.

sources.title: Chipping Sparrow Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
